# Team News: Argentina vs. Cape Verde injury, suspension lists, predicted XIs | World Cup 2026

July 2, 2026

Share

Group J winners Argentina return to World Cup 2026 action on Friday, when they take on Group H runners-up Cape Verde in the last-32 match in Miami.

The Blue Sharks have excelled on the global stage on their debut, securing two clean sheets in the group stage, while the defending world champions remain unblemished after securing three wins from three.

Here, Sports Mole rounds up the team news for both nations.

---

## ARGENTINA

Out: None

Doubtful: None

Sports Mole’s predicted XI: E. Martinez; Molina, Romero, Lisandro M., Medina; De Paul, Mac Allister, Fernandez, Almada; Messi, Lautaro M.

## CAPE VERDE

Out: None

Doubtful: Telmo Arcanjo (muscle)

Sports Mole’s predicted XI: Vozinha; Moreira, Pico, Diney, Cabral; Lenini; Mendes, Duarte, Monteiro, Cabral; Livramento

---

Previous article

Rashford transfer saga takes new twist as Man Utd are tested

# Argentina vs. Cape Verde projected lineups, starting 11 for World Cup Round of 32 game in Miami

Sahar Fatemi

•

SN

Add The Sporting News on Google

Argentina take on Cape Verde in Miami in a World Cup Round of 32 match that pits the defending champions against the smallest nation ever to reach the knockout stage.

Lionel Scaloni's side swept Group J with three wins from three, beating Algeria 3-0, Austria 2-0 and Jordan 3-1. Lionel Messi, in what is expected to be his final World Cup, sits among the tournament's top scorers.

Cape

# Argentina vs Cape Verde Preview: Predicted Lineups, Team News & Tactical Analysis | 2026 World Cup Round of 32

Argentina vs Cape Verde clash in Miami for their 2026 World Cup Round of 32 knockout. Predicted lineups, team news, tactical breakdown and score prediction.

Updated on July 3, 2026 2:17AM EST

Updated on July 3, 2026 2:17AM EST

# Argentina vs Cape Verde Preview: Predicted Lineups, Team News & Tactical Analysis | 2026 World Cup Round of 32

Lionel Messi and the defending champions against the smallest nation ever to reach a World Cup knockout stage. Cape Verde held Spain goalless in the group opener, drew every group game without conceding and advanced as one of the tournament's best third-placed teams on the back of three clean sheets. It is a romantic story, and they have earned their place here. Argentina are still going to win.

The holders come through Gro

# Argentina vs Cape Verde: Predicted Lineups, Team News and Match Prediction

By Dicip Khetan

Argentina 2026 World Cup X/@AFASeleccionEN

- Argentina face Cape Verde in the FIFA World Cup 2026 Round of 32 in Miami
- Lionel Messi and Alexis Mac Allister are expected to return to Argentina's starting XI
- Cape Verde are expected to rely on a compact defensive setup after an impressive group-stage campaign
- Argentina are favourites, but Cape Verde have already shown they can frustrate elite opposition

Defending champions Argentina will look to continue their FIFA World Cup 2026 title defence when they take on tournament debutants Cape Verde in the Round of 32 at Hard Rock Stadium in Miami on Friday.

Lionel Scaloni's side progressed through the group stage with a perfect record, winning all three matches to top their group. Argentina have looked balanced throughout the tournament, combining defensive solidity with the attacking quality of Lionel Messi and Lautaro Martinez, who continue to spearhead one of the strongest squads in the competition.

Cape Verde, meanwhile, have emerged as one of the surprise packages of

# Argentina vs. Cape Verde projected lineups, starting 11 for World Cup Round of 32 game in Miami

Sahar Fatemi

•

SN

Add The Sporting News on Google

Argentina take on Cape Verde in Miami in a World Cup Round of 32 match that pits the defending champions against the smallest nation ever to reach the knockout stage.

Lionel Scaloni's side swept Group J with three wins from three, beating Algeria 3-0, Austria 2-0 and Jordan 3-1. Lionel Messi, in what is expected to be his final World Cup, sits among the tournament's top scorers.

Lionel Scaloni's side progressed through the group stage with a perfect record, winning all three matches to top their group.
